entities.name: FARMER-MANAGED NATURAL REGENERATION
entities.description: A greening initiative and critical intervention for soil health and climate resilience involving the growth of indigenous trees.

entities.name: SOIL HEALTH
entities.description: The state of the soil being healthy, productive, permanent, and self-replenishing.

entities.name: CLIMATE RESILIENCE
entities.description: The ability of the land to withstand climate-related stresses.

entities.name: MAGARIA
entities.description: A department in the southern part of Niger where FMNR was implemented.

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: The country where the study on FMNR was conducted.

entities.name: SOIL EXPOSURE
entities.description: A state of severe land degradation due to low vegetation cover.

entities.name: VEGETATION COVER
entities.description: The proportion of the land surface covered by plants.

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A period of abnormally dry weather that FMNR helps the land withstand.

entities.name: INDIGENOUS TREE
entities.description: Native tree species fostered by FMNR to restore the ecosystem.

entities.name: EROSION
entities.description: The wearing away of soil, which is prevented by FMNR.

entities.name: FLOODWATERS
entities.description: Excess water that FMNR helps manage through better infiltration.

entities.name: SOIL INFILTRATION
entities.description: The process by which water on the ground surface enters the soil.

entities.name: NUTRIENTS
entities.description: Essential elements for plant growth ensured by FMNR.

entities.name: REMOTE SENSING
entities.description: The technology used to analyze the landscape recovery in Magaria.
